test.name: Wikipedia request should route to search-wikipedia
test.description: User explicitly names Wikipedia. That is search-wikipedia, not search-wiki. The two skills target different sources — Wikipedia is the general encyclopedia, the FamilySearch Research Wiki is the genealogy-method reference. search-wiki must not answer a Wikipedia request.

input.user_message: Look up Albert Einstein on Wikipedia and save the summary

negative.explanation: The user explicitly named Wikipedia and asked for the summary to be saved — that is search-wikipedia's exact workflow (it calls the wikipedia_search tool). search-wiki searches the FamilySearch Research Wiki via wiki_search and would return genealogy-method guidance, not the Wikipedia article the user asked for.

judge_context: Should route to search-wikipedia rather than calling wiki_search on a related query
